name: Willie Nelson
abstract: Willie Hugh Nelson (born April 29, 1933) is an American singer, guitarist, songwriter, actor, and activist. He was one of the main figures of the outlaw country subgenre that developed in the late 1960s as a reaction to the conservative restrictions of the Nashville sound.

about_quotes.speaker: Duffy Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: how do you reach those people that love Willie Nelson so much and you’re trying to influence them and enlighten them to another understanding?
about_quotes.doc_title: Duffy Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 17, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: Wynton and myself played with Willie Nelson. We played “Cal, Don’t You” with Willie Nelson, that was my first time to meet Willie Nelson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Laurence Juber
about_quotes.evidence: Well actually doing a session with the Great Gonzo was — it was Paul Williams, Willie Nelson, and the Great Gonzo all at once.
about_quotes.doc_title: Laurence Juber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Glasgow, Scotland, July 27, 2016 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel
about_quotes.evidence: I grew up with the Beatles, Motown, Glen Campbell, Willie Nelson, the Temptations, Rolling Stones.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019
